name: Weyerhaeuser
cat: Forestry & Building Products / Timberlands & Wood Manufacturing

political.s: FEC data shows Weyerhaeuser's political donations totaled $321K with a slightly Democratic lean (54.9% Republican, 45.1% Democratic), reflecting bipartisan engagement. The company lobbies on forestry, timber, and environmental policy matters affecting the forest products industry.

charity.s: Weyerhaeuser provided $5.9 million in charitable grants and contributions in 2025, distributed through the Weyerhaeuser Giving Fund to local communities where the company operates. Focus areas include affordable housing, education, environmental stewardship, and community development across the US and Canada.
charity.focus: Affordable housing, education, environmental stewardship, community development, disaster relief

environment.s: Weyerhaeuser reports sustainable forestry practices with certified timberlands, carbon accounting through its Carbon Record program, and ambitious climate targets to reduce Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 42% by 2030. The company emphasizes continuous harvest-regrowth cycles and carbon storage through wood products, though DitchCarbon rates it 44/100 for overall sustainability performance.

labor.s: Weyerhaeuser has reduced its recordable incident rate from over 10 in 1990 to 1.99 in 2024, demonstrating strong safety culture. The company offers competitive pay, benefits, paid parental leave (4 weeks), and has formal training and leadership development programs, though specific wage comparison to median worker pay not disclosed.

dei.s: Weyerhaeuser added inclusion as a core company value in 2019 and established an Inclusion Council to advance DEI initiatives. The company maintains voluntary diversity training with 100+ micro-learning options, eight employee resource groups, and conducts pay equity reviews. Leadership accountability and mentoring programs support inclusive culture.

animals.s: Not applicable; Weyerhaeuser is a forest products and timber company with no involvement in animal testing.

guns.s: Not applicable; Weyerhaeuser manufactures wood products and building materials, not firearms or weapons.

privacy.s: Weyerhaeuser maintains reasonable security safeguards for personal information and has privacy policies compliant with major frameworks (Safe Harbor, CCPA). However, the company reserves broad rights to monitor employee systems without privacy expectation and rents recreational agreement holder contact lists to third parties with approval.

execPay.s: CEO Devin Stockfish earned approximately $12.95 million in total compensation (2024), comprised primarily of bonuses and stock awards. Specific CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio not disclosed in available sources, but executive compensation appears elevated relative to typical worker earnings.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
